entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What's New in Q3 2024
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP is always evolving to better serve our customers. With the latest update in Q3 2024, in addition to quarterly content maintenance and a new product page we've made significant improvements to the user experience:

  1. New Side Menu for improved page navigation
  2. New UI Elements for additional context
  3. New Search Feature 

Read more about the details of these enhancements in this blog post.

This section details the new UX features added to the release navigator for SAP BTP.

New Side Menu for Easier Navigation

We've implemented a new side menu on every page of the release navigator to provide a more intuitive user experience. This menu allows easy navigation to the four new main pages of the release navigator:

  1. Product Specific
  2. General
  3. Get Assistance
  4. User Feedback

Fig 1. The side menu on all new pages

As a result, some content previously found on the Home page has been rearranged. Let’s walk through what you can find on each of the new pages.

Product Specific Page

This page serves as the starting point for the release navigator and corresponds to the former Home page. It contains the core of the release navigator: curated release information for specific products and services of SAP BTP as well as information on release schedules.

Fig 2. The Product Specific page, formerly known as the Home page

At the top of the Product Specific Page you can find a message tile with helpful information and a Release Calendar. The calendar includes release dates for SAP BTP as a whole, links to product-specific release schedules and dates for upcoming release navigator updates.

Below you can find the section Products. The cards in this section are sorted alphabetically and each one takes you to a dedicated page with in-depth information and curated resources on the 27 SAP BTP products and services covered.

Product Page Navigation

Fig 3. Example of a dedicated product page

Once you are done exploring the resources on a product page and want to move on to the next product or service, you can either click on Product Specific on the side-menu or on the back arrow on the left of the page title to get back to the Product Specific page and navigate to other pages.

General Page

On this page you can find General Resources that are helpful across all SAP BTP products and services. If you can't find a certain product or service on the Product Specific page, we recommend you check out the resources on this page (and also feel free to let us know in our survey).

Besides the General resources, there are two more sections on this page:

  • Additional Resources contains non-release specific but nevertheless important resources one should be aware of.
  • Release Navigators by SAP Enterprise Support provide direct links to all currently available release navigators for other solution areas.

Fig 4. The General page

Get Assistance Page

This page contains resources to help you use the release navigator and to improve your experience. You can access it from all pages in the release navigator by either clicking on Get Assistance in the side-menu or by clicking on the red bar at the very top of the page.

At the top of the page, you can find the following elements:

  • Release Navigator Enablement:
    • SAP Community Blog Posts: These blog posts go into detail about new features that are being added to the release navigator and provide other news.
    • SAP Partner Masterclass: This recorded digital session is only available to SAP partners.
  • Translation Feature: Allows you to translate the release navigator (not the pages linked to) into 40 languages. The translation process takes a few moments and may impact layout and design of the navigator pages. Click on this card to sign in to the authenticated version with Universal ID or an S-user account to use this feature.
  • User Feedback: Click here to provide feedback in a quick 3 question survey.

Below you can find the Navigation Tips, detailed instructions on how to use the release navigator, similar to the blog post you are reading now.

Fig 5. The Get Assistance page

User Feedback Page

We've embedded a feedback survey on this page with three short questions to gather feedback and suggestions for improvement.

  • Your relationship to SAP.
  • Whether you found the release navigator helpful.
  • An open text field that allows you to let us know anything you liked, what you would like to see added, or what you think could be improved.

We strive to always improve the release navigator, so we really appreciate any feedback you submit.

Fig 6. The User Feedback page

Helpful UI Elements

We added two new types of UI elements that you will find throughout the pages of the navigator.

Lightbulb icon

Many pages come with additional information to guide you through the cards available on that page and provide more context on when to use which resource.

If you see a lightbulb icon next to a header, you can hover over it with your cursor to open a text bubble with additional information about that page.

The bubble can be moved around by clicking and holding your cursor and can be closed by clicking the  on the top right.Fig 7. Demo of the lightbulb icon text bubble

On product pages, the information displayed in this bubble corresponds to the information formerly found on Release Guidance tiles.

About this Card

Some resource cards come with additional tips and contextual information.

If you see three dots on the bottom right of a card, you can open a tip with additional information about that particular card. To open the link of that card, you need to close this tip by clicking the three dots button again.

Fig 8. Demo on the About this Card tips

Due to the high demand for dedicated pages for products and services, the number of pages to navigate has grown significantly since launch of the release navigator one year ago and we aim to increase the number of pages even further in future updates. More pages mean more content but also more cards to browse, which can increase the time it takes to find what you’re looking for.

Our goal is for you to find what you need as quickly as possible. That’s why we implemented a new Search Feature, which allows you to search for keywords and find all pages associated with this topic, product or service name. Click on the  Search icon at the shell bar and a pop-up will open. Once you enter key words, you will see which pages are associated to this term and you can jump directly to them. The search feature is optimized for product and service names.

Fig 9. Short demo of the Search function.

We collaborated closely with SAP BTP experts to add a new page for SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery. This page contains links to the release schedule, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New Viewer in the SAP Help Portal and more. Check it out.

Fig 10. The new page for SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery.

Currently, the release navigator for SAP BTP covers content for 27 products and services on dedicated pages. For all products and services not featured with their own page you can find helpful resources on the new General page.

Expect more new pages dedicated to specific products and services in future updates and don't hesitate to let us know via our feedback survey, which products or services you would like to see added next. 

Last update, we announced the new SAP Note Consolidated Release Schedules for SAP BTP and the corresponding initiative of communicating the release schedules of SAP BTP products and services.

We are happy to report that the number of products and services who are communicating their release schedules has further increased to more than 50 now thanks to close collaboration with various SAP BTP teams.

Check the schedules here and from the Release Calendar section of the navigator.

Tip: Did you know you can mark notes as a favorite? If you do so, you will be notified via email whenever the note is being updated. This way you can stay on top of new release schedule communication, like new dates or skipped waves. We recommend you mark all those notes as a favorite that you are interested in receiving updates for.

Fig 11. Where to click to mark a note as a favorite and receive notifications once it gets updated.

Please note: To access SAP Notes you need to log in to SAP for Me using your Universal ID or S-user ID. Read more about the required authorizations here.

Next updates are scheduled on a quarterly basis in alignment with the Harmonized Release Calendar for SAP Cloud Products.*Disclaimer: The schedule is subject to change without prior notice including, when necessary, delays or incorporating changes in subsequent update windows and not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver any material, code, or functionality.Looking ahead, 2025 updates will mainly focus on quarterly maintenance of release resources and a steady extension of product coverage. Currently, there are no plans for further mayor UI updates in the near future.

The Readiness@Scale team at SAP Enterprise Support is dedicated to ensuring release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to be prepared and ready to adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have any feedback or specific requests for content in the release navigator, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

And don't forget to check out our team's blogs tagged with Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale 
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Perform MDG Derivation from Particular Attachment Document
entry.content:
Abstract

Change request attachments in SAP MDG play a crucial role in supporting various business needs. Attachments may range from technical documents to reports. In this scenario, the goal is to implement a derivation process that leverages the information from an uploaded attachment document. Its content provides values for the fields in MDG to be pre-filled upfront instead of manually maintaining them.

 

Introduction

In this blog post, we will provide a step-by-step guide to implementing a derivation process triggered by an attached document within an MDG-M change request. By enabling users to upload an Excel sheet containing the required values for specific fields, we introduce a more user-friendly and efficient way to handle data derivations. This approach is especially beneficial for large datasets, where manual input would be time-consuming and susceptible to errors. With this solution, the attachment serves as a basis for automatically populating fields, streamlining the process and reducing the risk of inaccuracies.

 

Scenario and Feasible Option

The main goal of this implementation is to derive data from an uploaded Excel sheet, which will be used to automatically populate relevant fields in the change request. The Excel sheet should include the following columns: entity type, attribute, and value. (An example of a completed Excel sheet will be provided in the Testing section of this blog.)

As a result of this implementation, it is also necessary to address the challenge of identifying the correct attachment document for the derivation process. To achieve this, we will extend the attachment UI by adding a new field called ‘Attachment Type.’ It will act as a filter for identifying the corresponding document during the derivation process.

Based on this approach, the process can be divided into three main steps:

  1. Extend the Attachment UI: A new custom field labeled "Attachment Type" will be added to both the "Attachment List" and the "Add File" UIBB. A new button, "Derive with Attachment" will also be created to trigger the derivation process.

  2. Identify Corresponding Document: Logic will be developed to identify the correct attachment by filtering based on the "Attachment Type" field. Enhancements will be made to the CL_USMD_CR_GIL_ATTACHMENT class to create post-exit methods, ensuring only the appropriate document is selected.

  3. Perform the Derivation: Derivations will be performed in the ‘GET_DATA’ method of the Attachment List UIBB's feeder class, and relevant messages will be handled in the ‘PROCESS_EVENT’ method.

 

1. Extend the Attachment UI

This part of the solution is adapted from the approach outlined in ‘MDG Change Request Attachments - Hide/Disable attachments in MDG CR based on User Role’. You can check this blog for further information about ‘Attachment Type’ extension.

An MDG change request may contain multiple attachments, making it necessary to differentiate between them. To address this, we will introduce a custom attribute called 'Attachment Type.' This attribute will then be added to the 'Attachment List' and 'Add File' UIBB. Beside these improvements, we will add a custom button ‘Derive with Attachment’. This button will trigger the main derivation code.

a- Create Dictionary Objects


Figure 1- Create domain


Figure 2- Add value for dropdown list box


Figure 3- Create data element

b- Append to Attribute Structure (BSS_CRIL_ATTACHMENT_ATTRIBUTES)

To add a new field to the Attachments section, we need to extend the structure for GenIL MDG CR Attachments. This can be done by navigating to the relevant structure and creating an append structure that includes the new data element.


Figure 4- Create append structure


Figure 5- Final view of BSS_CRIL_ATTACHMENT_ATTRIBUTES

c- Add Field to USMD_CR_ATT_FILE (‘Add File’ Form UIBB)

First, we will customize the Form UIBB of the ‘Add File’ popup. Navigate to the configuration of USMD_CR_ATT_FILE and add the newly created 'Attachment Type' field to the form.


Figure 6- Add ‘Attachment Type’ field to Form UIBB

d- Add Field to USMD_CR_ATTACHMENTS (‘Attachments’ List UIBB)

Then, we will customize the List UIBB of the ‘Attachments’ list. Navigate to the configuration of USMD_CR_ATTACHMENTS and add the newly created 'Attachment Type' field to the list.


Figure 7- Add ‘Attachment Type’ column to List UIBB

e- Add ‘Derive with Attachment’ Button


Figure 8- Add button to toolbar



2. Identify the Relevant Document

Once the "Attachment Type" field is available, logic will be developed to identify the correct attachment for derivation. The GenIL Object Handler Class for MDG CR Attachment will be enhanced with post-exit methods to ensure the document with the correct attachment type is selected.

a- Create enhancement for CL_USMD_CR_GIL_ATTACHMENT


Figure 9- Sample Excel sheet filled with right format

Since this class is a final class, we can’t inherit and modify the required methods. Instead, we will create an enhancement implementation and insert post-exit.

b- Create post-exit to method CHANGE_OBJECT

In this part of the code, we retrieve the attributes of the changed attachment and check the value of the "Attachment Type" field. If the attachment type is identified as "Derivation Document" (with the code 'DER'), we mark it for derivation. This value is then stored in memory to be used in the subsequent derivation process. If the attachment does not match the required type, it is excluded from the derivation logic. This ensures only relevant documents are processed during the change request.

c- Create post-exit to method GET_ATTRIBUTES

In this section of the code, the attributes of the attachment are retrieved, and based on a flag set in the previous method, the attachment type is classified as either "Derivation Document" or "Other Documents." If it is a derivation document, its attributes are stored in memory and sent to the feeder class for further processing. This ensures that the system can distinguish between documents that are relevant for derivation and others that are not, maintaining the integrity of the process.

 

3. Perform the Derivation

The derivation process will occur in the GET_DATA method of the attachment list UIBB’s feeder class, where the Excel sheet will be read, and the field values will be derived accordingly. Messages related to the derivation process will be handled in the PROCESS_EVENT method, ensuring clear feedback is provided to the user.

a- Create custom feeder class for Attachments List UIBB

The CL_USMD_CR_GUIBB_ATTACHMENTS class, which is the feeder class for the Attachments List UIBB, needs to be enhanced. Therefore, we can create a custom feeder class and use CL_USMD_CR_GUIBB_ATTACHMENTS as its superclass.


Figure 10- Custom Feeder Class

 

b- Redefine IF_FPM_GUIBB_LIST~GET_DATA

We will begin by retrieving the attachment structure stored in previous methods. An attribute will be used to verify the existence of an attachment with the correct type.

A crucial component of this code is the CL_FDT_XL_SPREADSHEET class. We will utilize specific methods from this class to select and read the relevant worksheets. Once the worksheet data is read, it is essential to map these values to the appropriate fields.

After completing the data extraction and mapping, we will use the WRITE_ENTITY_DATA method from the CL_USMD_CONV_SOM_GOV_API class to write the entity data.

 

c- Redefine IF_FPM_GUIBB_LIST~ PROCESS_EVENT

This method is used to display warning or error messages in unexpected scenarios. For instance, if the "Derivation Document" type of attachment is not uploaded but the derivation button is pressed, this code will trigger a warning message.

 

Outcome - Testing

To validate this implementation, we will upload three different documents. One of these documents will be an Excel sheet with field values that need to be derived. This document will have "Derivation Document" selected for the "Attachment Type" field, ensuring it is used for the derivation process. After document uploads, we will click ‘Derive with Attachment’ button. Only files that comply with the specified structure will be processed, while those that do not match the structure will be ignored.


Figure 11- Sample Excel sheet filled with right format


Figure 12- Add documents and click button


Figure 13- A part of the derived fields


Figure 14- Displayed warning message when there is no derivation document uploaded


Figure 15- Displayed error message when there are multiple derivation documents uploaded

 

Conclusion

This blog has presented a practical approach to executing derivations in SAP MDG based on an attached document. By incorporating a custom field for attachment types and automating the derivation process, we can greatly minimize manual data entry and enhance operational efficiency. This solution is designed to be both scalable and flexible, making it adaptable to a wide range of business requirements and ensuring long-term usability.

 

Thank you for reading.
Best regards,

Merve, Çınar

entry.title: Is your business ready to tackle your data challenges?
entry.content:
Flexible approaches to data management such as a business data fabric still require well-managed data as a foundation. A semantic layer of active metadata and visual representations of data assets such as knowledge graphs are only as good as the quality of the underlying data.  Similarly, generative AI that is trained on corporate data is reliant on the underlying quality of that data, just as a house relies on sound foundations.  As such, corporations are exploring ways to maturing their data management practices.  However, every organization is different, and obviously there’s no one perfect way to invest in data management. Even so, it’s useful to have insights into the best practices being leveraged by your peers to calibrate what your current (and future) investments in these areas look like.

When it comes to broader data management initiatives, those business deploying best-in-class (BIC) data management practices see higher organization performance as compared to the average.

Best-in-class data management organizations vs. industry average organizations performance

Want to see where you stand?  Here is a quick, six-question online Aberdeen assessment you can leverage to gain high-level insights into how your organization’s broader data management strategies compare to those of your peers.  Upon completion, you will receive a report tailored to your organization and how it compares to others in Aberdeen’s research.

Click here to get started! 

We hope the report is informative and provides you with some insight into the building blocks that make up a strong data management strategy.

Finally, here are some additional resources that can assist you on your data management journey:

  • Learn about what a business data fabric is, including how it can benefit your business:  https://www.sap.com/documents/2023/08/02af33dc-887e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html
  • Discover a step-by-step framework to implementing a business data framework: https://www.sap.com/documents/2023/10/4675c6a3-927e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html
  • Find out what's needed in a modern master data management (MDM) system:
    https://www.sap.com/documents/2024/08/8aff13da-d17e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html
  • Explore data management solutions on sap.com:  https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/analytics.html

entry.title: Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 02
entry.content:
Latest Innovations in a Nutshell

Continuing the blog series about updates on MDG innovations in SAP S/4HANA, I'd like to provide you with details on the latest innovations in SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 02. Highlights include:

  • Generative AI for assisted changes/summary of changes in central governance (classic mode) and for business partner processing (in cloud-ready mode) using Joule AI copilot; only available with SAP S/4HANA 2023 Private Cloud Edition (not on premise)
  • Improved data quality analytics for business partner and product master data.
  • Enrichment of business partner from CDQ reference data​ as an integrated part of consolidation, mass processing, etc.

These come in addition to various continuous improvements in central governance (classic mode).

Innovations in Detail

Let's take a closer look at the innovations.

Inclusion of Generative AI in MDG

Generative AI is used for summary of changes and assisted changes in central governance (classic mode), and for optional business partner processing (in cloud-ready mode) initiated by a Joule dialog. 

The AI-generated summary of changes provides you with an overview of changes in natural language (using a large language model) enabling you to: ​

  • verify the completeness and/or correctness of master data changes.​
  • get a brief overview of the changes comprised in an MDG change request.​
  • avoid pointless revisions based on incomplete changes.​
  • have a non-technical summary of changes (in contrast to change documents).​
  • facilitate approver’s final checks.

Generative AI-based summary of changes

​The AI-assisted change lets you change master data attributes with as little effort and as few steps as possible so that applying changes to a master data object is time efficient. You can make prompts in natural language to make field changes.

The AI-assisted change in central governance is applicable for business partner and material, and the AI-generated summary of changes in central governance is applicable for business partner, material, and custom objects which do not use editions.

Generative AI-based features in master data processingTo get a glimpse of the AI-based features in central governance (classic mode), watch the short video.

In addition, SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA integrates with the AI copilot Joule, enabling queries in natural language and navigation to SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA cloud-ready mode for follow-up actions on business partner (incl. supplier and customer) data. (This innovation can be consumed as of FPS01).

Joule integration with follow-ups in MDG (cloud-ready mode)

These initial steps of including Generative AI in MDG processes are planned to be continued to cover more use cases in MDG classic mode and cloud-ready mode.

 

Note:  The AI features outlined above come only with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (not with the on-premise release).

"Data Quality Analytics 2.0" for business partner and product master data

  • SAP Analytics Cloud comes with an enhanced dashboard for chief data officers or master data specialists to get a real-time quality abstract on the prevailing quality and history trends of product and business partner master data, providing:
    • data quality overview in 3-level reporting structure by category, dimension, and rule
    • data quality analytics for seven business partner segments, e.g., Business Partner Core Data, Customer Sales Data, and Supplier Purchasing Data, each with drill-down using master data attributes or/and organization unit e.g., Country, Company Codes etc.
    • data quality analytics for seven product master data segments, e.g., Plant data, Sales Data, and Classification Data, each with drill-down using master data attributes or/and organization unit e.g., Product Group, Plant etc.
    • embedded authorization control via live data connection
    • integration in MDG system for data remediation

Data quality overview in 3-level reporting structure: by category, dimension, and ruleDeep-dive data quality analytics on business partner in SAP Analytics Cloud

For more details about "Data Quality Analytics 2.0", read the SAP Community blog post by Kefeng Wang.

  • Analytics CDS views for master data quality of business partner and product provide data source (CDS views) for master data quality analytics of business partner and product using organizational unit and attributes in SAP S/4HANA.

Enrichment of business partner records from reference data as an integrated part of consolidation, mass processing, etc.

In cloud-ready mode, the enrichment of business partner records from CDQ reference data is enabled as an integrated and automated step within:

  • Single Processing​
  • Mass Processing​
  • Consolidation​
  • Federation processes

Mass enrichment of business partners with reference data in SAP S/4HANA

For more information about the enrichment of business partner records from reference data, check the overview presentation and read the SAP Community blog post by Martin Steinhauser.

Integration of SAP Business Workflow as local workflow engine (cloud-ready mode)

As an alternative to BTP Workflow Service (subscription required), the SAP Business Workflow can be assigned to process steps in the process template configuration. It can trigger any custom-defined action after a process step (e.g. email notification).

Integrate SAP Business Workflow as local workflow engine (cloud-ready mode)

Continued improvements for central governance in classic mode (material data)

Increased effectiveness through enhanced copy function in search and use of validation rules for classification in central governance for material: 

  • You can copy a selected material from the Search Materials Result List and choose one of three different copying options: Only Basic Data, All Data, or By Organizational Levels. When selecting Only Basic Data, basic data, long texts, and classification are copied. Choosing All Data will result in the complete data from the template material being copied. If you select By Organization Level, a dialog box will appear allowing you to choose which organizational data should be copied.​ 

Copy function in material search
Additionally, if a change request exists for the template material, the changed data from staging will be included in the copied material.

  • You can use validation rules for classes (table KSSK) and characteristics (table AUSP), defined using the rule management functionality of MDG, data quality management to check data during change request processing.

Validation rules for classes and characteristics

Continued improvements for central governance in classic mode (business partner incl. customer and supplier data)

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 comes with improved Partner Functions content display and relationship categories in business partner processing:

  • The relationship type allows you to classify the relationship category into different subdivisions. Accordingly, you can maintain the relationship type on the user interface for Relationships, Contact Persons, Shareholder Relationships, and their related details user interface.
  • In addition, there are more search criteria when using the F4 Help on the Partner field in the partner functions lists ERP Customer Sales Areas: Partner Functions or ERP Vendor Purchasing Organization: Partner Functions. The F4 help also includes Business Partner, Customer, Supplier and Maximum No. of Hits as search criteria. The UIBBs for Partner Functions include the column Assigned BP. 

UIBB Partner Functions

Maintaining the Relation Type

For an overview of the innovations outlined above, see also the What's New in SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS 2 presentation. As you can see from these innovations, both classic mode in MDG and cloud-ready mode are continuously enhanced. The driver for innovations in classic mode is primarily the SAP Continuous Influence program, whereas cloud-ready mode in MDG is the strategic development path going forward.

To get the full picture of planned innovations in SAP Master Data Governance across the entire MDG solution portfolio up to 2025, see the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

entry.title: Deep Insight of Your Master Data with MDG Data Quality Analytics 2.0
entry.content:
Abstract

SAP Master Data Governance is a state-of-the-art master data management application. Data Quality Management in MDG enables you to define master data quality rules, striving for consistent usage across all points of entry and enabling data quality monitoring and remediation.

On release SAP S/4HANA 2023FSP2 and Cloud 2408, SAP Master Data Governance has delivered data quality analytics 2.0, which enables end users, from Chief Data Officer to master data specialist, a real time quality abstract, and the data quality drill down further by important segment e.g., rule attribute and important business partner/customer/suppliers/products attributes and organization unit for example Country, Plant, Sales Organization,  Company Codes etc..  

It includes mainly:

  • 14 Analytics CDS views for product and business partners
  • 2 Out-of-box data quality dashboards for product and business partners in SAP Analytics Cloud by leveraging these analytics CDS views.

Highlights:

Data Quality Analytics 2.0 provides Quality Score on dimensions for example, master data  attribute, organization unit etc., used in the dashboard. It gives you a clear comparison between organization units for example plant, company code etc., on same data quality standard. Each Plant, Sales Org, Region owner have clear picture of their own data quality status and ranking among all organizations. From there, they are motivated to identify a problem, dig root cause, and finally put it into action to improve data quality.  From aggregated data quality score, it is also possible to drill-down into each erroneous master data to trigger data remediation process.

 

Analytics CDS Views

See 7 analytics CDS views for Business Partners and 7 analytics CDS views for Product  in SAP Help Portal 

All these analytics CDS views support custom fields extensibility on business partner, customer, suppliers, and products master data. 

In S/4,  end user can open view data of analytics CDS in graphic or reporting UI using Query Browser app. Key user can also configure smart business apps for multiple end users using Manage KPI and Report app.

Query Brower - MDG Data Quality CDS ViewsQuery Browser - Customer Sales Data Quality Analysis

Understand the Quality Score

Each analytics query CDS view provides a measure Quality Score, which is calculated dynamically for your selection dimension attribute.

The Quality Score give you clear comparison between organization units e.g., plant, company code on same data quality standard.  Each Country, Plant, Sales Org, Region owner have clear picture of their own data quality status and ranking among all organizations.

Data Quality Score by Country/Region

 

Data Quality Dashboards 2.0 in SAP Analytics Cloud

The 2 dashboards provide a deep quality analytics for business partner(including customer and supplier)  and product master data which can quickly identify the root cause of quality problem and continuously monitoring data health to safeguard customer core business. It enables end users, from Chief Data Officer to master data specialist, a real time quality abstract, and the data quality drill down further by important segment e.g., rule attribute and important business partner/customer/suppliers/products attributes and organization unit for example Country, Plant, Sales Organization,  Company Codes etc.  

Both Dashboard contains an Overview page providing data quality score on following reporting: 

  • Dimension Category 
  • Dimension 
  • Validation Rule 

Dashboard for Business Partner( SAP__MDG_DQ_BP_V2)  contains an Analytics page providing quality score and number of Not OK evaluation results for following data: 

  • Business Partner General Data 
  • Customer General Data 
  • Customer Sales Data 
  • Customer Company Code Data 
  • Supplier General Data 
  • Supplier Purchasing Data 
  • Supplier Company Code Data 

Dashboard for Products( SAP__MDG_DQ_PRODUCT_V2)  contains an Analytics page providing quality score and number of Not OK evaluation results for following data: 

  • Product Header data 
  • Product Plant Data 
  • Product Sales Data 
  • Product Description 
  • Product Valuation Data 
  • Product Classification Data 
  • Product Dimensions Data 

Both dashboards provide embedded authorization control via live data connection of analytics CDS views in MDG on S/4. And also provides integration to MDG system for data remediation seamlessly. 

SAP Analytics Cloud - Data Quality Dashboard 2.0 - BP: Overview

SAP Analytics Cloud - Data Quality Dashboard 2.0 - BP: Analysis

 

SAP Analytics Cloud - Data Quality Dashboard 2.0 - Product: Overview

SAP Analytics Cloud - Data Quality Dashboard 2.0 - Product: Analysis

Set Up Dashboard for Your SAC Tenant

Open Content Network from your tenant, find the SAP Master Data Governance – Data Quality Analysis package, and import it.

You can find details in my previous blog:  Get Your Master Data Quality Insight from SAP **bleep**... - SAP Community

SAP Analytics Cloud - Business Content Network


 

Summary

Following this blog, you should be able to

  • explain what’s provided functions in MDG Data Quality Analytics 2.0.
  • set up the out-of-box data quality dashboard in SAP Analytics Cloud for your end user or using S/4 embedded analytics capabilities.

entry.title: 🚀Fire of Innovations: Highlights from SAP TechEd 2024 📢
entry.content:
The SAP TechEd 2024 event, themed “Fire of Innovations,” has once again set the stage for groundbreaking advancements in enterprise technology. Held virtually on October 8-9, this year’s conference showcased a plethora of new tools, updates, and strategies designed to empower businesses and developers alike. Here’s a deep dive into some of the most exciting announcements and innovations from the event.

1. Joule: The AI Copilot Revolution

One of the standout highlights was the unveiling of new capabilities for Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot. Joule has been supercharged with collaborative AI agents that can perform complex, cross-disciplinary tasks. These agents are designed to break down silos within organizations, enhancing productivity and allowing human workers to focus on more creative and strategic activities.

For instance, in dispute management, autonomous AI agents can now analyze and resolve issues such as incorrect invoices or duplicate payments. In financial accounting, these agents streamline processes like bill payments and ledger updates, quickly addressing any inconsistencies.

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Enhancements

SAP continues to expand its Business Technology Platform (BTP), which is central to its cloud strategy. This year, SAP announced the expansion of BTP coverage through eight new SAP-managed data centers. Additionally, the SAP Discovery Center now offers a Quick-Account-Setup feature, enabling businesses to deploy specific use cases with just a click.

The integration of ABAP Cloud into SAP Build was another significant update. This integration allows developers to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby, ensuring a seamless and efficient development process.

3. Innovations in SAP Build

SAP Build has been strengthened to support a “Clean Core” strategy, which is crucial for maintaining system consistency and upgradeability. The SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, introduced for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, helps both key users and developers create on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

4. Harnessing the Power of Data with SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP introduced the SAP Knowledge Graph, a next-generation solution that connects SAP data with rich business context. This tool is poised to help developers unlock the full value of SAP data, driving more informed decision-making and innovation.

5. Global Reach and Accessibility

This year’s SAP TechEd was a truly global event, with a free two-day virtual format that included keynotes, technical sessions, and developer enablement activities. The event was designed to be accessible to a worldwide audience, ensuring that everyone could benefit from the latest SAP innovations.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 has once again demonstrated SAP’s commitment to driving innovation and empowering businesses with cutting-edge technology. From AI advancements with Joule to enhancements in SAP BTP and SAP Build, the “Fire of Innovations” theme truly encapsulates the transformative potential of these new tools and strategies. As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of the digital age, SAP’s innovations provide a robust foundation for growth and success.

Stay tuned for more updates and insights from SAP TechEd as we continue to explore the future of enterprise technology.

Feel free to share your thoughts or ask any questions about the event! What innovation are you most excited about?

entry.title: MDG Custom entity refresh on change, Empty custom entity
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to pass on my experience from a recent upgrading event; I'm documenting it here so that anyone who encounters this particular issue will have a reference and a solution to address it.


In MDG, building a custom entity is a very simple and well-documented process; unfortunately, after our system upgrade, custom entities ceased showing information or maintaining data on changes in NWBC. 

I recreated tones of custom entities and subsequent UIBBs on screen, but their behavior was similar across all custom entities. I checked every component of the GenIL or MDG models, as well as every custom enhancement or handler class.
After spending hours and hours trying to figure out the problem, we discovered that SAP had recently made corrections for performance optimization, one of which was an authorization check on entities. 

The change was applied to standard handler classes, specifically in method "IF_MDG_BS_BP_ACCESS_HANDLER~CHECK_AUTHORITY_BY_ENTITY". This method is now obsolete and all logic is moved under "IF_MDG_BS_BP_ACCESS_HANDLER~CHECK_AUTHORITY_MASS_BY_ENTITY". However, the fix included a warning message in the obsolete method, which caused authority checks to fail, causing data refresh issues on custom entities.

This fix is in SAP Note 3406917.
I hope this helps someone save a lot of time on debugging.

entry.title: Mass enrichment of business partners with Tax and ID numbers found using the CDQ data provider
entry.content:
Abstract

In this blog post you'll find information about the "Reference Data Lookup" step that can be put into process templates for the Manage Governance Processes Business Partners app. The "Reference Data Lookup" step automatically enriches business partners with Tax and ID numbers found with the help of the third-party data provider CDQ. If the automatic enrichment is not successful, the user can manually follow up with the Look Up Reference Data for Business Partners app.

 

Prerequisites for using the Reference Data Lookup step as a customer

  • SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 Feature Pack Stack 02
  • Contract with CDQ AG, see SAP Store
  • Using Cloud-Ready Mode
    Below you can find a basic comparison of cloud-ready mode and classic mode for orientation. See Comparing Cloud-Ready Mode and Classic Mode for more details.

  • Follow the setup Configure Reference Data Lookup Step

 

In a nutshell

The image below shows you the result of the Reference Data Lookup step within a consolidation process in the Manage Governance Processes Business Partners app. In addition to that also mass, single and federated processes are supported. By choosing the "Show Details" button the user sees which BP (business partner) was changed and which number was added - in this case VAT DE342757812.

 

Overall, the Reference Data Lookup step has the following qualities:

  • Works out-of-the-box
    After the setup, including adding the user and password from CDQ, no business rules need to be added to make the step work
  • Works automatically
    The step is intended to run through without user intervention.
  • Enrich as many business partners as possible while not enriching false-positives
    By applying the strategy of careful matching and enriching, the functions avoids matching to a wrong company and enriching incorrect details.
  • Uses CDQ's golden record
  • Adding only missing Tax and ID numbers while respecting system checks

 

Step Logic

Within the step the following diagram shows in a simplified way, one ideal path (in pink) for processing individual business parners. The actual, more detailed flow would show how that simple flow is different for mass processing (for example, business partners are collected and sent together in batches to avoid too many requests) and the split of responsibility within the process between SAP and the data provider CDQ.

 

 

In the above image you see that not only business data (second green box), but also the origin of the business data (first green box) is added into the BP as Reference Record Links. Reference Record IDs stored in the BP are called Reference Record Links. Here is an example showing the corresponding section in the Manage Governance Processes Business Partners app:

 

 

There are 2 ways to create such a link – both ensuring the high quality of that information:

  • As a user using the Look Up app to choose the right Reference Record and then creating a link. Manually typing in such a number in the BP is not possible.
  • In the background, during the Reference Data Lookup step the link is created by the system.

The Reference Data Lookup step trusts this link over other business data. If the business partner contains a reference record link, the data provider should return the current data for this linked reference record and not search to find a better match to another reference record.

In the example above (from the very beginning), the "4 ID numbers added across 2 BPs" contain 2 business numbers (one being Commercial Register Number - BUP002, HRB 20552) and 2 Reference Record Links (one being Corporate Data Quality - DPI001, GR.DE.RC:B8536_HRB722007).

 

DEMO

See below a video for different ways to work with the results of the Reference Data Lookup step. Click the image or follow the link Reference Data Lookup step in action.

The following files have been used in the video and are publicly available with note 3166696 - This note stores files for MDG demo scripts in SAP Cloud Appliance Library: 

  • the file
    "20241210_154324_Mass_enrichment_business_partners_by_external_Data_Provider.xlsx" is the same as in the video at minute 13:00, under the file name
    "BusinessPartner20240904_143824_5german_1US_inclRef.xlsx"
  • the file "Mass_enrichment_business_partners_by_external_Data_Provider - examples and data flow within step.pdf" describes what happens with the different business partners, for example "A Z GmbH" in slide 2 fits to what is shown in the video at minute 16:40

     

Other related information

  • Blog Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 02 talking about MDG wide latest innovations in a nutshell
  • Blog Efficient Mass-Enrichment of Business Partners into SAP MDG: A Comprehensive Guide from our development partner CDQ
  • the "Reference Data Lookup" step can be tried out handson on SAP Cloud Appliance Library with the help of the following test case Perform Mass Enrichment of Business Partners with Tax and ID Numbers Found Using CDQ Data Provider.
    This test case can be found amongst others within the blog SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Demo Guides, under the section title Master Data Governance, MDG or directly with clicking on the following link Master Data Governance, MDG. 

Author

Martin Steinhauser, Product Owner for Master Data Quality Services focussing on Data Provider Integration topics.

entry.title: Discover TechEd “Live” at SAPinsider EMEA 2024
entry.content:
Following hot on the heels of the hugely successful SAP TechEd Virtual event last month, attention now turns to a number of live events that will feature TechEd content and speakers, starting at the ASUG Tech Connect event in West Palm Beach, the first of SAP’s “TechEd on Tour” locations, as well as featuring in sessions at the SAPinsider EMEA “TechInsider” event in Copenhagen. Both events run from 12- 14 November in these wonderful, if quite different, locations.

I’ve been deeply involved in the planning for the SAPinsider event again, and for this reason alone will focus my blog on that event, though I am hopeful that both events will deliver a rich experience of TechEd content for attendees, and allow them to delve deeply into SAP technologies, particularly relating to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Artificial Intelligence (AI).

At SAPinsider Copenhagen we’re going the extra mile, in trying to deliver a TechEd-style experience for attendees, who I’m happy to say will not only find an agenda littered with TechEd sessions, delivered by many of our stellar TechEd speakers, but also they’ll be able to participate in multiple hands-on sessions as well as having an opportunity to continue their learning experience by networking with SAP’s Developer Advocates who will guide and support attendees as they work through a number of the tutorials available at the Developer Garage on the show floor – which we are pleased will make its very first appearance at this SAPinsider event.

With all this on offer I’m excited for what’s in store in Copenhagen next week – and I hope that you are too!

Plan Ahead – And Set Your Agenda Now!

For those of you registered for the event, and in a manner which is not unlike visiting a Disney theme park, my suggestion for you to get the most out of an SAPinsider event is to plan ahead – not so much to beat the queues as you might at the theme park, but more so to make sure that you see all you want to see, and achieve what you set out to do. And the best way to do this is to prepare your agenda for the event before you go.

The first step is of course to get the event app, “SAPinsider Events”, available on the App Store and Google Play, and once you’ve installed that you’re good to go in terms of adding sessions to your personal agenda. Now for the fun bit – what sessions to see? Perhaps I might help with some ideas.

I’ll assume that everyone will plan to join the day 1 and day 2 keynotes. I’m looking forward to both, the motivational first day keynote from Hein Wagner, starting at 9.00am on Tuesday 12th November, followed the next day by the AI inspired second day keynote, featuring Martin Böhm of Ottobock and Jonathan Von Rüden of SAP, and which starts at the same time of day on the 13th November.

But after the keynotes, what next? Here are my personal 6 TechEd inspired picks for Copenhagen:

  1. Enhancing SAP Development Processes with SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Interoperability to Elevate Your Extensions, 12-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  2. Hands-On Lab: Develop Applications and Processes with SAP Build and Joule, 12-Nov-24, 15:30 - 17:30
  3. Mastering SAP Business AI for Enhanced Performance and Innovation, 13-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  4. SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere Solutions: Integration, 13-Nov-24, 14:15 - 15:00
  5. Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, 13-Nov-24, 16:45 - 17:30
  6. SAP BTP Strategy and Highlights, 14-Nov-24, 09:00 - 09:45

Of course, this is just the tip of the iceberg, as there are many more sessions available for you to select from the agenda, and among which you’ll find more TechEd content, as well as content from other speakers from SAP and our business partners, in addition to many customer case stories too. And in a shameless plug too, you may also note my session “Navigating Your Path to Success with SAP BTP” which I shall deliver alongside Nis Boy Naeve on 13 November at 11:30am. Please join us if you can! Take a look at the SAPinsider event website sessions list, or the event app that you’ve already downloaded to see more.

Your Continued Learning Experience

If you’d like to continue your own personal learning journey after the event, then why not explore other learning opportunities from SAP? For example, learn how to build software applications, side-by-side extensions, and integrations to and from cloud applications by exploring SAP’s free learning content on SAP BTP. It is made for both integration designers and extension developers from all levels of expertise and will help you stay up to date with the latest SAP BTP innovations. And check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on SAP Learning site.

Or if you’re interested in TechEd sessions, but can’t join the events in West Palm Beach or Copenhagen, then perhaps another TechEd on Tour location, in Australia, Germany or the UK would work better for you?

For those of you travelling to Copenhagen next week, I do hope that you find SAPinsider EMEA 2024 to be a truly informative and stimulating experience. And, of course, if you visit the show floor then look me up at the SAP exhibit booth, and I’ll be happy to have a chat with you about SAP BTP, the conference or just about the amazing city of Copenhagen!

My best wishes for safe travels, and I hope that you enjoy the event!

Chris Grundy

Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: SAP Master Data Governance: A Leader Among Users
entry.content:
SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) has consistently been rated as a Leader in the Fall 2024 G2 Grid for Master Data Management (MDM) on the G2 peer to peer review site, and the reasons are clear.

First and foremost, SAP MDG offers a comprehensive and intuitive platform for managing multi-domain master data across the enterprise. With its user-friendly interface and robust capabilities, organizations can easily create, update, and maintain their business-critical master data in a centralized and controlled environment, while leveraging 3rd data quality and enrichment services to automate the process.

One of the key features that sets SAP MDG apart is its ability to streamline data governance processes and ensure data quality and consistency. The platform offers a wide range of data governance capabilities, including data validation, standardization, correction, enrichment, and consolidation, which helps organizations maintain high-quality and accurate master data thru a single lens.  The solution also provides data quality management, enabling organizations to define master data quality rules, striving for consistent and validated data across all points of entry while also enabling data quality monitoring and remediation.

Another key feature of SAP MDG is its tight integration with other SAP solutions, which allows organizations to leverage existing data models, business logic, and validation frameworks. This integration not only streamlines data management processes but also enhances collaboration between different departments by providing a unified view of master data. Furthermore, the application supports open integration with third-party products and services, ensuring that organizations can maintain a flexible and adaptable technology stack. Its combination of centralized governance with decentralized ownership through federation, along with seamless integration capabilities, positions it as a valuable asset for any enterprise aiming to optimize its data landscape and drive better business outcomes.

Lastly, the robust support and training resources offered by SAP further contribute to the platform's high user satisfaction ratings. Users appreciate the strong customer support and extensive training materials that are available to help them maximize the value of SAP MDG within their organizations.

Overall, SAP Master Data Governance's ability to centralize and streamline master data management processes, its seamless integration with other SAP applications, and its strong support and training resources all contribute to its high user satisfaction. As more organizations recognize the importance of effective master data management, it's no surprise that SAP MDG continues to be a leader in the master data management market space among users.

Learn more:

  • SAP Master Data Governance on G2
  • SAP Community for SAP Master Data Governance

entry.title: What's New in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2411?
entry.content:
SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition is a software-as-a-service (Saas) solution on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that is used to govern core attributes of business partner data (more domains planned). It is perfectly suited for organizations kick-starting a master data management initiative at low entry barriers. For details, see also the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition overview and  deep-dive presentations.

What's New?

Continuing the blog series about innovations in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition, I'd like to provide you with details on the latest innovations with SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2411. 

General Remark

Generally, a new version of several existing apps is available to provide new functionalities, for example, simulation of validation rules and additional process attributes. You can recognize the previous versions that have now been deprecated by the Deprecated label on the tiles. Note that a user can only use either all deprecated or all new versions when working with SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition. We recommend using the new version of all apps as soon as possible, as the deprecated versions will be removed in the next release.

Updates in Data Quality Management

Data quality management comes with new apps to support derivation scenarios, validation rules and analytics, and a new version of the validation rules app. 

New and enhanced apps in data quality management

In detail, the increased scope in data quality management for business partner data, enables master data specialists to:

  • Define derivation scenarios
    • With the new Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners app, you can create and edit derivation scenarios for your business partner data. You use derivation scenarios to deduce master data based on rules and a defined scope to ensure data quality.
  • Import/export of derivation scenarios
    • With the new Import Derivation Scenarios app, you can import derivation scenarios and their rules, which you previously downloaded using the Export Validation Rules app. Validation rules, their business details and their BRFplus implementation can be imported.

Importing derivation scenarios - log details

  • Import/export of validation rules
    • With the new Import Validation Rules app, you can import validation rules, which you previously downloaded using the Export Validation Rules app. Validation rules, their business details and their BRFplus implementation can be imported.
    • With the new Export Validation Rules app, you can download validation rules, which are managed in the Validation Rules for Business Partners app, to then import them into another system. Validation rule exports contain validation rules, their business details and their BRFplus implementation.

Exporting validation rules

  • The new version of the Validation Rules for Business Partners app offers the following new features:
    • Copy validation rules
    • Simulate validation rules to check if they work as intended and to get a preview of the rule results
    • Check if the rule is assigned to any dimensions, which is done in the Configure Data Quality Scores for Business Partners app descibed in the following:
    • The new Configure Data Quality Scores app allows key users to define their data quality reporting structure by using Categories and Dimensions. The aggregation of rules scores to data quality dimensions and categories is as follows:
Categories
  • Multiple categories supported
  • Score: Average of dimension scores 
  • Thresholds: Target, Warning, Critical 
Dimensions
  • Multiple dimensions supported 
  • Score: weighted average of rule scores 
Rules
  • Assigned to dimension with Impact
  • Multiple assignments possible, but only once per category

 Improvements in Business Partner Hierarchy Management

New features in Track Changes – Business Partner Hierarchies and Track Changes – Business Partner Hierarchy Nodes apps

  • A value help was added to the Business Partner Hierarchy and Business Partner Hierarchy Node fields. In the Track Changes - Business Partner Hierarchy Nodes app, you can search for a hierarchy ID or hierarchy description to find the associated nodes.

New features in new Manage Business Partner Hierarchies app

  • The following new features are available in the new version of the Manage Business Partner Hierarchies app:

    • The node ID is now generated automatically if you leave the field empty. The generated node ID is based on the hierarchy ID and a counter.

    • The system can generate node descriptions automatically. To generate one, add a business partner to a node and choose Accept. The first and second word of the business partner will be used as node description.
    • The search filters Business Partner and Hierarchy Node ID are now available. These filters can help you to find the associated hierarchy.
    • You can now edit a hierarchy that is in a process in the Process Information tab. In this tab, you can change the Process Description, Process Template, Priority, Due Date, and Reason.
    • When you make changes to your hierarchy, you can now see the Node Status column in the app. Edited nodes are displayed as Changed or Inserted. Deleted nodes disappear from the hierarchy.

Additional Improvements in Business Partner Processing

Additional innovations include:

  • With the new My Governance Processes - Business Partner app you can display business partner processes that have been started by you. The system displays the list of processes as well as the number of business partners, BP relationships and hierarchies.

My Governance Processes app showing all running processes initiated by oneself

  • With a new feature in the Maintain process attributes for Manage Business Partners and Manage Business Processes apps you can now maintain special attributes such as priority, due date or reason to categorize and analyze master data processes when you create or change a business partner. This information can be changed as long as the process is not activated or cancelled.

Categorization of master data creation process by priority, reason, and due date

For an overview of the innovations specified above, see also the What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition 2411

Continued innovations in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition help organizations in managing their core business partner data master for their entire system landscape. 

To stay on track, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, read the MDG-related blogs, and join the  Q&A forum.

Best,

Markus

entry.title: Benefits of SAP MDG Solution
entry.content:
Benefits of SAP MDG Solution

Here are the key benefits of SAP's Master Data Governance (MDG) solution

  • SAP MDG performs effective master data management. It helps to improve data quality, consistency, a

     

    nd accuracy. Many industries use SAP MDG to increase customer experience, reduce costs, enhance productivity, and speed up new product launches.
  • Flexibility is one of the key benefits of SAP MDG. SAP MDG allows customization. It is a centralized platform that helps manage master data across various domains such as HRM, ERP, CRM, and supply chain.
  • SAP MDG supports managing both structured and unstructured data. So you can manage customer and product data, emails, and so on.

Central Governance

You can create and maintain master data with SAP MDG. You can streamline master data using a central system that adheres to data rules and standards. With this approach, master data is maintained in a central system and then replicated in satellite systems. 

It ensures that the downstream system transactions leverage master data, as shown in the below image.

Know that SAP MDG manages the master data through a clear audit trail. It provides significant business benefits, improving business operations.

SAP MDG helps to avoid error-prone manual maintenance on master data. It offers centralized governance and data replication to systems. Moreover, it ensures data consistency and maps master data across entire enterprise systems.

 

Consolidation for Analytics Purpose Only

Master data is usually created in standalone or transactional systems based on needs. You must consolidate and centralize the master data for data analytics. It can be done by merging and mapping the data to standard data. The image below explains the same.

It enables organizations to easily manage and maintain a single version of the truth for all their critical data. 

This approach allows organizations to gain valuable insights and make informed decisions with accurate and reliable data.

 

 

Consolidation for Initial Load Before Central Governance

It is necessary to merge and create the best record to consolidate master data in the central governance scenario. You can achieve the same by using the consolidation functionalities of SAP MDG. It is usually a one-time activity in which companies manage their master data and replicate it into multiple systems.

The consolidation scenario helps to extract, cleanse, and consolidate data during the initial load. It helps prepare the existing data for central governance, ensuring it is accurate reliable, and adheres to data rules and standards. 

With SAP MDG, organizations can streamline this process, enabling them to easily manage and maintain their master data and replicate it across multiple systems.

 

Consolidation for Mergers or Acquisitions

Introducing new systems into an enterprise's landscape can create data harmonization and duplication challenges.

However, companies can synchronize and de-duplicate new data with the existing data using the consolidation functionalities of SAP MDG. Using SAP MDG, enterprises can merge and map data to a common data standard, ensuring that new data is consistent and accurate with the existing data. This helps avoid the creation of duplicate records, ensuring that the data remains clean and reliable. 

By using SAP MDG's consolidation functionalities, organizations can streamline harmonizing and de-duplicating new data. It enables organizations to quickly integrate new systems and leverage the full potential of their master data.

Continuous Hybrid Approach

If you want to refine master data from source systems, you must combine the consolidation and central governance scenarios. It helps to manage the enhanced data using a significant governance process. 

SAP Master Data Governance offers several functionalities to support various use cases, including:

  • Business-driven workflow and workflow rules framework to automate master data management
  • Reducing implementation costs by reusing data validation as well as mapping logic.
  • Integrate with SAP data model for key master data domains
  • Customize master data objects' configuration frameworks to optimize data management.
  • Provide extension capabilities for standard master data objects
  • Provide APIs and integration capabilities to access data and use change logs.
  • monitoring data management processes for built-in master data stewardship tools
  • managing data quality through business rules repository
  • analyzing data quality for product and master records
  • correcting data quality issues through worklists and embedded analytics

 

 

Differences Between SAP MDG and SAP MDM

Here are the key differences between MDM and MDG:   

Criteria

MDM

MDG

Definition

It ensures the quality, consistency, and accuracy of data across the enterprise

It provides a way to manage master data in multiple systems while maintaining consistency

Data Storage

You can store data in one central location

You can store data in multiple systems

Data Distribution

Data is distributed from the central location to other systems

Data is replicated across multiple systems

Single Version of Truth

Provides a single version of truth for data

Provides consistency for data stored in multiple systems

Use Case

Useful when you need to synchronize data across the organization

Useful when you need to maintain data consistency across multiple systems

 

 

                                                                                                                                            Published By

                                                                                                                                            Nikhat Fatima

entry.title: Automate your core Master Data creation processes with SAP MDG cloud edition and Derivations
entry.content:
Hi,

Some great news for the SAP BTP SAAS service “SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition“! As officially announced on this page What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition:

SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition now supports DQM Derivation Scenarios.

Many MDG experts already know this capability from the SAP MDG Private cloud edition and know the strength of this feature.

 

Introduction

SAP has now released the following Fiori Apps in SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition in that space:

  1. Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners
  2. Import Derivation Scenarios and Export Derivation Scenarios

With the app “Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners” you can now implement (no code) business rules to derive business partner data automatically in a background step of a MDG process. The following SAP standard process templates include the “derive step”:

  • SAP_BP_COA SAP: Consolidate BP Automated
  • SAP_BP_MA SAP: Mass Processing BP Automated
  • SAP_BX_COA SAP: Consolidate BP+Rel. Automated
  • SAP_BX_SA SAP: Single Processing BP Automated
  • SAP_BX_DCA SAP: Decentr.Create BP+Rel.w/Mtchg Autom
  • SAP_BX_DUA SAP: Decentr. Update BP+Rel. Autom.

Before: Process without derivations

Business example without derivations:

 

Step 1: Before the automation was defined the business end users used MDG cloud edition to create new Business Partner manually.

Step 2: Automation - In the past the end users had the chance to derived BP data like Name, Tax Number and Address data from an external repository like CDQ. That automation already increased the process performance and optimized the data quality

Step 3-6: Manual activities - After that step the end users had to manually create some more BP data like BP-Roles, assignment to Industry sectors and company codes and organizational data (Sales Area for the customer part and purchasing data for suppliers)

Step 7: Finally the end user can submit the request.

Summary: The whole process took a reasonable amount of time and was pretty error-prone because sometimes the end users forgot to assign some entities.

After: Process with MDG Derivations:

Same result as above but with derivations:

 

Step 1: Business end users uses MDG cloud edition to trigger a new creation process for a Business Partner.

Step 2: Automation - As before: MDG cloud edition is doing a lookup of BP data like Name, Tax Number and Address data from an external repository.

Step 3: Submit request – End user doesn’t have to enter more master data attributes because they will be derived in the next step

Step 4: Automation – The MDG Process will execute the derivation scenario. By this the business rules are applied within seconds and no user input required.

Summary: The new process with derivation will decrease the time to create new master data and in addition in a higher data quality.

Scenario Details

Screenshots from the design time app “Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners”

1. MDG Derivation Scenario level

Comments:

  • In Derivation Rules for Fields: Derive single values for attributes on level BUT000
  • Derivation Rules for Tables: The scenario will derive (create new table lines) for various tables like BUT100, BUT0IS, MDC_KNVV, MDC_KNB1, MDC_LFB1 and MDC_LFM1
  • For each of the tables a rule is created.
  • Each of the rule can be managed/governed individually by an status based process.

2. Details for the derivation rule “Derive Industries for Organizations”

 

Comments:

  • Condition Fields (eg BUT000-TYPE) can be used to define additional conditions on rule level. In this example you can define different rules for different conditions. An example is that an BP of type Person gets different Industry Sectors (or no?) assigned compared to organizations.
  • Result Fields are the attributes which are derived by the system according to the decision table (see next screenshot)

3. Details for the BRF+ Decision table for Industries

Here you can review how to define the business rule without coding in BRF+

 

If the BP is of type Organization, the system will derive 2 new Industries for it. No ABAP coding with MDG API required. In a SAAS solution BADI coding option is not available anyways.

4. Details for the BRF+ Decision table for Company Codes

Comment:

The column 1 "Condition Decision Table for TABLE" is maintained in the "Condition table" to have more flexibility. In this example "DUMMY"  means that for all BPs (executed in the process and within the scenario condition expression) the two Company Codes entries will be derived. Since newer version of DQM Derivation Scenarios this DUMMY is not need anymore. 

Screenshots of the new process with derivations

1. Start the creation process

 

Comments:

  • With this app you can create a new BP from scratch, or you use the “Look Up and Create” feature.
  • You can also search for existing BPs and send them into a change process.

 

2. Retrieve the lookup data from external data provider

 

Comments:

  • See help: https://help.sap.com/docs/mdg-ce/administration-guide/integrating-reference-data-for-business-partners
  • You do a search on the data provider within the MDG UI and select the correct BP in the list. By using the “Create Business Partner” button the data will get copied to your request form. It will take over all the data from the provider like address, organizational names, Tax and ID Number.

3. Submit the request

 

Comments:

  • The end user can leave the entities for Industries, BP-Roles and organizational data empty because the system will derive the data after the submit.

 

4. Review the automatic derivation

This is the UI Inbox task as presented to the approver:

 

Comments:

  • The derivations have been successfully executed and the system defaulted the values for Organizational information. Also the BP-Roles are derived.

This is a screenshot of the derivation step in the “Manage Governance Processes” App:

 

Comments:

  • You can see that the derivation affected 7 tables.
  • The green color identifies that new rows have been added. For BP-Roles you can see that 4 new rows (BP-Roles) have been added.
  • In this app you can do further drills downs to see all the details for the derivations.

 

5. Check the record created by process after automation.

The following huge screenshot highlight in red all the data which have been derived by derivation and in green the derived values from the data provider look up. In this example process the system was able to derive >95% of the data without any end user input.

 

Next steps for you

If you are interested in this feature I recommend checking the following documentation:

  • What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition - Help
  • SAP Master Data Governance - Community
  • SAP MDG How To Guides
  • Blog from @Sabine_Stellma https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/automate-your-master-data-process-with-derivation-scenarios-in-sap-master/ba-p/13538990

With the updates on Q4/2024 for the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition the SAAS solution got some more interesting features. Please read this What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition

You can also test MDG cloud edition as a FREE TIER: Follow this guide to get you hands onto MDG cloud edition.

Cheers
Steffen

++ Many thanks for reviewing the content of this blog to @Sabine_Stellma and @Mark63  ++

Video of the end user process: https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_yhdkgjr2

entry.title: Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance
entry.content:
Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance 

We are excited to announce the release of the new version of the Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance (MDG), which has recently been published on Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

This updated version includes new solution variants for SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA in classic mode and in cloud-ready mode. One of the key highlights of this release is the inclusion of detailed diagrams for business partner, product, and financial domain for master data governance in classic mode. These diagrams provide a comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, making it easier for users to understand how MDG fits into their existing IT landscape and how it can be integrated with other systems and applications.  

Updates in Detail

1. Solution Variant “Federated Master Data Governance with Core Data Owner on SAP S/4HANA”

We added the information that Federated Master Data Governance can also be used with MDG on lower releases/classic-mode. More information can be found in the how-to guide and in the blog "Consumption of Federated Governance Made Easy" from @Mark63.

In the next two solution variants, we have replaced the existing one for “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA” with two new solution variants to illustrate the supported processes: 

2. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Classic Mode”

In the Solution Value Flow Diagram, you can see that MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode supports multiple domains, including business partner, product, and financials. This diagram provides a clear representation of how a business process variant is implemented and supported by a specific solution. 

As example you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners. In the case of Decentral Creation, a consolidation process is used, whereas for Decentral Change, the MDG, Central Governance Change Request is used. 

The descriptive text next to the diagram contains more information: It provides details about the standard delivered process template and change request types that are suitable for decentralized creation and change processes.  

3. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Cloud-Ready Mode”

In comparison to the Solution Variant for MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode you can see the Solution Value Flow Diagram for cloud-ready mode currently only contains the Business Partner Domain. 

The Solution Process Flow Diagrams were initially published last year. We have now included three new diagrams, which demonstrate the support for Mass Load, Mass Change, and Mass Deletion of Business Partner Relationships. 

Furthermore, to draw a comparison with classic mode diagrams, here you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners in cloud-ready mode. In both cases, consolidation processes are used. 

We believe that this latest release will be a valuable resource for organizations looking to implement or expand their MDM capabilities. By providing a clear and comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, we aim to empower users to make the most of their MDM investment and to achieve their data management goals more efficiently.  

We invite you to explore the new Reference Solution Architecture for MDM by yourself on the Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

Further information

Curious about the initially released content and about the different diagram types such as Solution Value Flow diagram, Solution Component diagram, Solution Data Flow diagram, Solution Process Flow diagram? 

  • Check the blog post “Explore Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance” from my colleague @KarinBlank. 
  • Watch our Video: How to use Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance - YouTube 

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, join the Questions & Answers forum and Blog space, and check the MDG roadmap for all MDG versions. 

Best, Christina Lindner

entry.title: Efficient Mass-Enrichment of Business Partners into SAP MDG: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
Uploading trusted business partner master data into SAP MDG is easy. Simply connect your MDG to your First Time Right connector, enter an address, choose your preferred data source, and populate your change request. Simple, right? But what about real-world scenarios where you’re not starting a business from scratch and need to manage an existing business partner base?

What happens when you need to repeat this process 100,000 times for all of your business partners? Do you enter 100,000 addresses, choose the best match 100,000 times, download the data 100,000 times manually, and create 100,000 change requests?

At this scale, an easy process quickly becomes impractical, likely overwhelming your master data department’s resources for months. And by the time they’re finished, the data may have already changed, forcing the process to start all over again.
